programme.title: Capítulo 200
programme.desc: Diego tells Roberta he's ready to confront his dad about her and the band. Gastón hears Celina throwing up. Tomás feels like his life is no longer enjoyable. Roberta decides to stay with Simon and not forgive Diego. Franco and Alma fight over Peter. Lupita tells Mía that she won't be going to Orlando. José and the others decide to hire an escort for Alicia.

programme.title: Capítulo 201
programme.desc: It's revealed that Celina has bulimia. Roberta beats up Pilar for saying Madariaga is leaving. Madariaga tells the kids he's leaving. The girls find an escort. Diego, Giovanni, and Tomás get Simon's number and find out he's married. Nico convinces Miguel to show Gastón he is on good terms with Franco. Pilar tells everyone Madariaga was pushed out. The kids go on strike for Madariaga. Alma tries to gather information on Simon. Gandia expels the scholarship students. Renata interrupts Madariaga's meeting to bring him back to the school.

programme.title: Capítulo 202
programme.desc: Mayra scolds the students who walked out of the protest. Madariaga offers his resignation in exchange for the kids not to be expelled. Diego mentions Simon's wife in front of him and Roberta. Madariaga's project is denied. León learns that the members of RBD go to Elite Way School. Giovanni and Tomás find Simon's address. After León's visit, Gandia awkwardly interrogates Diego. Diego gets Roberta to meeting Simon's wife.

programme.title: Capítulo 203
programme.desc: Franco tells Mía that Miguel refuses to accept the house or stay in Mexico. Hilda sees Alicia with Popeye. Alma and Franco once again fight over Peter. Yuli tells Nico she's not going on the family vacation. Mía asks Gastón to tell her what he knows, later he gives her an envelope with evidence against Miguel. Pilar tells Roberta that Diego will give up RBD names. Alma and Diego decide to work together to expose Simon; she sees Simon with his baby. Madariaga rather have the kids angry at him than tell the truth. The kids literally turn their back on Madariaga. Gandia thinks Hilda ordered the escort. Mía reflects on her precious moments with Miguel.

programme.title: Capítulo 204
programme.desc: Gandia calls out the RBD members for expulsion. Mía tells Franco Miguel's dad died because of him and Miguel came here for revenge. Diego's plans to help the band are revealed. Franco tells Mía that Carlo was responsible for Miguel's dad financial ruin and subsequent death. Simon tells Roberta he has a son. Franco grants Gastón permission to protect Mía from Miguel. Gastón threatens Miguel. Celina has a bulimic scare. Carlo returns to Mexico. Mía confronts Miguel about his revenge; the most explosive scene of their relationship.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Capítulo 207
programme.desc: Alma tries to make Franco see the truth about Valeria however, her evidence gets ruined. Gastón checks to see if José is registered for next year. Miguel confronts Mía about the money. Franco proposes to Valeria. Gastón continues to harass José. Some of the students things go missing. Celina progresses with the doctor. Miguel struggles with the truth of his father's death. Franco tells Mía he's marrying Valeria in a week. José leaves school. Gastón hits Miguel and Roberta defends him; Gastón chases her down.

programme.title: Rebelde

programme.title: Episode #5.10
programme.desc: The victim's name was Dale Harrell. He was married for ten years and was soon to be a partner at his company as a successful mechanical engineer. He made 83, 000 a year and provided a safe home for his wife and three children in Gilbert, Arizona. At approximately 2;36 AM on January 14, 2009, his wife of ten years, Marissa DeVault, put a hammer in his husband's head five times while he was fast asleep, only hours after having sex. Her first 911 calls to the Gilbert Police Department claimed abuse and rape. The ensuing investigation revealed a story known only to a few and a life kept secret from a husband. There was a roommate with a gun. There was a secret life of a millionaire lover. And, from her days as an exotic dancer, Marissa attempted to hire her former lover and bouncer of a strip club. The killer was not a visitor to the club and she was not a bartender or server. Instead, she could be found on the stage as an entertainer. How did all these characters come together to result in the death of Dale Harrell? Where did the gun come from and how was it instrumental in revealing the truths of a killer? Who was her "Sugar Daddy" and was he an accomplice? Why was the hammer used to kill instead of a gun when the gun was only mere feet of the woman accused. It is a story filled with twists, turns, characters and lust and, in the end, a victim lay dead. His name was Dale Harrell.

programme.title: Elote el guajolote
programme.desc: Vanesa recibe la visita de un inspector de seguridad en su clínica para cerciorarse de que todo esté en orden. Además, los vecinos aconsejan a Germán para conquistar a Virginia.

programme.title: Nos cayeron los chahuiztles
programme.desc: Casimiro Pérez, el hermano de Magda llega de visita. Además, la madrastra de Lorena le trae una noticia que cambiará su situación económica.

programme.title: Adopción
programme.desc: Cris shows Pedro the adoption application but they will receive a visit from the social worker. Germán, on the other hand, will be pruning the trees outside the building, without imagining the problems that this would bring.

programme.title: Buzón de quejas
programme.desc: Los problemas en el edificio revivieron un buzón de quejas que ya estaba olvidado, pero este buzón traerá problemas a Luis y Silvia. Por otro lado Morris esta a punto de reprobar el año.

programme.title: Can guash
programme.desc: Pedro se aterra por la llegada de su suegro pues tiene miedo de que no se lleven bien, además Germán y Vanessa emprenden un nuevo negocio.

programme.title: Oficinas de gobierno
programme.desc: Los vecinos aprovechan la apertura de unas nuevas oficinas de gobierno para emprender sus negocios y obtener algo de dinero, pero cuando Luis los descubre, intentará correrlos de la entrada del edificio.

programme.title: Llegada de Martina
programme.desc: Cris y Pablo están esperando a su hija Martina y, nerviosos por conocerla, preparan todo para recibirla. Por otro lado, Liz y Vanesa abren su tienda de ropa virtual y logran hacer un trato con Don Arturo.

programme.title: El gorrión de la canción
programme.desc: Dulce arrives at the building looking for the author of some songs to compose a new single for her. Also, Luis and Silvia meet the parents of one of their son's classmates.

programme.title: Alejandra godín
programme.desc: Arturo llega con la noticia de que Alejandra será una nueva empleada en la empresa donde trabaja. Por otro lado, una pequeña apuesta entre Germán y Birote crece sin control.

programme.title: Subasta
programme.desc: Magdalena se da cuenta que puede sacarle dinero a los objetos viejos de Arturo y se da a la tarea de hacer una subasta, carísima por cierto.

programme.title: Frankie publicista
programme.desc: Vanesa desesperada por salir de sus deudas, busca la ayuda de Frankie para diseñar la publicidad de su negocio, pero al final se da cuenta que el de las ideas realmente era otro.

programme.title: Oriundas
programme.desc: Magdalena receives an unexpected visit, a visit that will expose her past in Angangueo. Alejandra and Rocko try at all costs to take advantage of Magda's friend, who has always been in love with Arturo.

programme.title: Huele a muerto
programme.desc: Los vecinos están preocupados porque en el edificio hay un olor a descompuesto que provoca que todos sean sospechosos. Frankie y familia temen que la desaparición de Rocko esté ligada a que los López Pérez están comiendo carne.

programme.title: El carro de Don Arturo
programme.desc: Luis se ha vuelto obsesivo con la limpieza y Silvia intentará hacer algo para que se le quite la obsesión. Además, los López Pérez se han ganado un carro de lujo y lo presumirán a todos los vecinos.

programme.title: La ley del Godín
programme.desc: Alejandra descubre que en su trabajo, su padre es el mandadero y todos sus compañeros lo tratan mal. ¿Crees que Alejandra lo defenderá o también lo tratará mal? Además, Germán se siente mal desde hace unos días y no sabe qué enfermedad tendrá.

programme.title: Foto tatarabuelo
programme.desc: Luis recibe la visita de su tía Enriqueta, quien le regala un cuadro de su tatarabuelo pintado por Diego y Frida. Al compartirlo con sus vecinos, solo recibe críticas. Germán tiene un ataque de celos porque Jorjais tiene un nuevo mejor amigo.

programme.title: La real sociedad
programme.desc: Andrés Vaca llega al edificio para pagar un favor y los vecinos aprovechan su visita para organizar un equipo de fútbol, mientras que las vecinas se organizan para ser la porra del equipo.

programme.title: Bodón loco
programme.desc: La trabajadora social descubre que Pedro y Cris no están casados, por lo que, para conservar a su hija, deberán organizar la boda en 24 horas. ¿Crees que con la ayuda de los vecinos podrán realizar un bodón loco?

programme.title: Desazolve
programme.desc: Neighbors face problems with water pressure and need to unblock the pipes, but opt to invest the money in lottery tickets. Morris, on the other hand, is influenced by reggaeton.

programme.title: Ruido fantasma
programme.desc: Los vecinos escuchan un ruido estremecedor en el edificio y sospechan que es un fantasma, por lo que deciden realizar un ritual para expulsarlo. Mientras tanto, Morris busca empleo.

programme.title: Nos cayó la chaviza
programme.desc: Uno de los departamentos está en renta y Sofi, una chava muy positiva, tiene interés en rentarlo. Pero, ¿crees que los vecinos accedan a tener una nueva inquilina? Por otro lado, Luis y Silvia tendrán diferencias por Sandro, la ex pareja de Silvia.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Karoline Leavitt: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 38th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the MAGA mouthpiece, Karoline Leavitt. From “growing up in a small business family” to her college broadcasting days, to being the ultimate tradwife hypocrite, to shapeshifting into an obsequious Trump a** kisser, this may be one of our most irritating guys yet!

programme.title: Pod Save America (80-Year-Old Man Loses War)
programme.desc: Trump ends his war against Iran without accomplishing any of the goals he set at the war's onset. Jon, Tommy, and Lovett discuss how real the deal to end the war actually is, how it's playing out in MAGA world, and its implications for the economy and the midterms. Then, they react to the corruption at the UFC fight at the White House and Gov. Gavin Newsom's announcement that he and his wife are being targeted by Trump's Justice Department. Finally, Sen. Mark Warner talks to Tommy about the situation in Iran and Trump's nominee for Director of National Intelligence.

programme.title: Pod Save America (LIVE(ish) from the Obama Presidential Center)
programme.desc: Jon, Lovett, Tommy, and Dan record from Chicago after the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center, reacting to Barack and Michelle Obama's speeches, what it felt like to walk through the museum for the first time, and what lessons Democrats in the Trump era can take from Obama. Then, they react to the emerging details of Donald Trump's MOU with Iran, which he signed at the Palace of Versailles, JD Vance's efforts to seem like a normal person during his book tour, and why the algae in the Reflecting Pool is such an apt metaphor for Trump's presidency.

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (How Billionaires Are Destroying the Media (with Joy Reid))
programme.desc: The media landscape in the US continues to undergo drastic transformations, with billionaire ownership and corporate consolidation leading to mass layoffs and content censorship. This week, Stacey is joined by Joy Reid, host of the Joy Reid Show and the first black woman to host a primetime program on cable news. They talk about the layoffs at the Washington Post, why she transitioned into independent media, what is lost when Black journalists are no longer at the table, and what people can do to inform themselves in an era where billionaires with agendas control the news.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Graham Platner Isn't Backing Down)
programme.desc: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate running for Senate in Maine, stops by the studio to talk with Jon about Trump’s impending conflict with Iran, the future of Medicare for All, and what community organizing in rural Maine taught him about building political power in our polarized era. The two discuss new polls showing Platner leading Janet Mills in the Democratic Senate primary, how his tattoo controversy has resonated with Maine voters, and what he wants to change about the Democratic Party to rebuild a winning, working-class coalition.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (Lisa Rinna Opens Up About Her Traitors FEUD with Colton Underwood)
programme.desc: Lisa Rinna takes the Lovett or Leave It stage to take us behind the scenes of Traitors - and explain what happens when the inner Housewife is released.

programme.title: Strict Scrutiny (Why Is SCOTUS Hoarding Opinions?)
programme.desc: Leah, Melissa, Kate, and special guest Elie Mystal of The Nation speculate about why the Court is hoarding opinions this late into bad decision season before diving deep on the ruling in United States v. Hemani, which features drugs, the Second Amendment, and Amy Coney Barrett’s iconic Ambien jar. Also: games are played, a new summer cocktail makes its debut, and no holds are barred, especially when it comes to Trump’s flop makeover of the Reflecting Pool. This episode was recorded live at the Gramercy Theatre in New York City.

programme.desc: Picking apples while on a picnic, Oliver runs into the feisty Ira Hatch, the owner of the apple orchard. However, once back in town, Mr. Drucker tells Oliver that Hatch has been dead for years. An incredulous Oliver agrees to see a psychologist.

programme.title: Eb Returns
programme.desc: After his elopement and cross-country honeymoon, Eb returns to Hooterville. But his new wife is nowhere to be found! After sending postcards from all across the country, Eb has to confess that his fianc� stood him up at the alter on their big day.

programme.title: Not Guilty
programme.desc: Someone has stolen $300 from Mr. Drucker's general store, and it looks like Eb is the culprit. How else could he have bought himself a new car that cost, you guessed it, exactly $300? But that is just the first twist of the many turns of this story.

programme.title: How To Succeed In TV Without Really Trying
programme.desc: Lisa awakes, saying she's dreamt that she must invest in copper. When Oliver turns that idea down, she seizes on the opportunity to invest in a ten-year-old genius's electric company. Twenty-eight dollars buys her a third of the business.

programme.title: Arnold, Boy Hero
programme.desc: Arnold, the Ziffels' television-loving pig, has taken on a paper route to save enough money for a new color set. But when bank robbers show up and threaten his dreams, and Lisa and Oliver are held captive in their own home, Arnold has to fight back.

programme.title: Flight To Nowhere
programme.desc: The farmers of Hooterville are headed to France! Or at least they are if they can get organized. As part of an agricultural exchange program, the farmers are offered a chance to join a chartered government flight to Europe.

programme.title: My Mother The Countess
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is delighted to learn that her Hungarian mother is planning a visit to their little farm. Much to Oliver's (Eddie Albert) disbelief and dismay, a rumor spreads all around Hooterville that Lisa's mother is a European countess.

programme.title: The Spring Festival
programme.desc: Love is in the air as the first day of spring arrives in Hooterville. And who better to lead the town in celebrating it than Lisa. She is resolved to show Oliver just what spring is all about and sets out to produce a Spring festival celebration.

programme.title: Our Son, The Barber
programme.desc: It's a hair-raising episode as Eb (Tom Lester) enrolls in a correspondence course to become a barber. Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Eddie Albert) give him the money to learn the trade but learning how to cut hair by mail has its glitches.

programme.title: Oliver's Jaded Past
programme.desc: For a lawyer, Oliver should be more careful about what he signs. Lisa reminds him that two years ago Oliver made her a promise to take her back to New York for a two-week vacation. And she has it in writing� on the bottom of the kitchen drawer.

programme.title: The Hungarian Curse
programme.desc: It's hard to know who has a curse over Oliver, but he sure has his hands full with Lisa and her fellow Hungarian Lazlo Broslav. Lazlo appears with a letter claiming he once saved the life of Lisa's uncle, and now her family must repay the debt.

programme.title: The Rutabaga Story
programme.desc: There's a problem in Hooterville: no farmer seems able to grow a cash crop on account of the quality of the soil. But as it turns out from Oliver's (Eddie Albert) soil and mineral research, the oft misunderstood rutabaga is an ideal choice.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 1)
programme.desc: Green Acres goes Hollywood in this first installment of a two-part season finale. When Arnold the pig appears in a play at the Hooterville Theater, Lisa (Eva Gabor) arranges for an old friend to give Arnold a chance on the big screen.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 2)
programme.desc: It's a swine-sational third season finale for Green Acres, as Oliver and Lisa chaperone Arnold, the Ziffel's talented pig, to Hollywood for his big screen test. There's only one problem, Lisa failed to mention that Arnold is not your normal actor.

programme.title: Guess Who's Not Going To The Luau
programme.desc: Hooterville is abuzz as the townsfolk enter a contest sponsored by Peterson's Poi Juice, with a trip to Hawaii as the top prize. But no one expects the winner to be Arnold, the Ziffels' talented pig. Maybe a pig might fly after all, to the islands!

programme.title: The Rummage Sale
programme.desc: A charity rummage sale asks for donated clothing. Being among the wealthier residents of Hooterville, the organizers look to the Douglases. It isn't long before Oliver has to stop Lisa from giving away his entire wardrobe!

programme.title: Eb's Romance
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Eb is quickly engaged to Loreli Appleby, whom he has only known for a day. Eb tries to impress Loreli's father, a farmer, but in typical fashion, he accidentally ruins his future father-in-law's cabbage crop.

programme.title: Morticia & Gomez Vs. Fester & Grandmama
programme.desc: Because she thinks Fester (Jackie Coogan) and Grandmama (Blossom Rock) are spoiling the children, Morticia (Carolyn Jones) hires a governess. Insulted, Fester paints a white line down the center of the Addams house, dividing it in half.

programme.title: Fester Goes On A Diet
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez see Fester conferring with TV muscleman Jack LaGrann (Jack LaLanne), and think that Fester is planning a television career. However, Uncle Fester confides to Lurch that he has to go on a secret diet and exercise.

programme.title: The Great Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez find Grandfather Pegleg's treasure map and summon Captain Grimby. They hope to charter his boat for the treasure hunt. But after meeting the Captain, Morticia is careful to keep the nature of the mission secret.

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: Cat Addams
programme.desc: Dr. Marvin P. Gunderson is summoned to examine Kitty Kat, the ailing lion pet of the Addams Family. Morticia decides that it is the doctor who needs help. To build his confidence, human members of the family pretend to be ill so he can affect cures.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.desc: Mistaken for a rustler and a killer, Jed Cooper is hanged on the spot by crooked lawman Captain Wilson. But as Wilson and his gang flee the scene, there's one important detail they've overlooked…Cooper is still alive! Saved by a sheriff, Cooper takes on the job of deputy marshal to bring justice to the Oklahoma territory…and to the nine men who "done him wrong."

programme.title: Michael Cimino's Heaven's Gate
programme.desc: This lavish, epic western retells the true story of Wyoming's infamous Johnson County War, a brutal, late 19th century conflict during which wealthy cattlemen, backed by the U.S. government, hired mercenaries to murder 125 immigrant settlers.

programme.title: Breakheart Pass

programme.title: Chato's Land

programme.title: How Airbnb Scaled from 3 Guests to 2 Billion

programme.title: The Biology Of Focus, Success & Long-Term Energy
programme.desc: From personal dramas to scientific uproars, it's been a wild year for the biggest health & fitness podcaster in the world. And today we get to discover his biggest new insights about life, relationships and protocols. Expect to learn whether you actually should drink coffee within 90 minutes of waking up, how to get the best sleep of your life according to the latest science, how to become a morning person, what Andrew has learned about the perils of fame and public scrutiny, what new research says on the world of longevity supplements, why you should always do your research when testing with peptides and much more…

programme.title: The Lost Art of Reinventing Yourself
programme.desc: Expect to learn what “Don’t half-ass it” means, the story of how Matthew got his iconic starting role in Dazed & Confused, how to see the upside during any crisis, why having a sense of humour should be your default emotion, McConaughey’s own version of his Lonely Chapter, when you should listen to your gut versus your head, why McConaughey turned down $14.5M to pursue something great, Matthew's reflections on the 10 year anniversary of Interstellar, lesson on finding the perfect partner, and much more…

programme.title: You Were Never Taught How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Hustle culture often tells us that working harder is the only way to succeed—grinding through 80-hour weeks on 4 hours of sleep per night. But what if the real solution isn’t about working harder, but finding balance? What if true success lies in slowing down, savouring life, smoking a cigar on the porch occasionally and being fully present. How differently might we define success then? Expect to learn who Dry Creek Dewayne’s is, his backstory and upbringing, how to un-harden yourself, how any person can overcome & control their anger, what Dewayne has learned from working with horses, the dangers of being out of balance in work & life, how to have a better relationship with the voice in your head, how to find the right partner & why men need to learn to treat women better, why bad fathers are partly to blame for the downfall of America’s culture, what a day in the life of Dewayne’s life is like and much more…

programme.title: We’re Living in a System Built by Lunatics
programme.desc: These days, making sense of the world is harder than ever. From billionaires reshaping governments to debates over whether modern car chassis can support the average obese person. There’s no shortage of absurdities. Luckily, Tom has plenty to say about them. Expect to learn why Gen Z is more likely to skip the gym and use Ozempic instead, if Tom can and will reset his Instagram algorithm, why Gen Z is more likely to drink less and smoke more, why the younger generation is sexless than most, Tom’s thoughts on Elon’s current rampage through government bureaucracy, Tom’s recent obsession with True Crime documentaries, why being competent is a curse and much more…

programme.title: Paradise

programme.title: The Light Brigade
programme.desc: On a desperate mission to survive, a young military cadet (Will Wheaton) learns the difference between heroic ideals and the bitter realities of war as he and his dying comrades prepare a strike against the aliens who may have condemn them to death.

programme.title: Out Of Body
programme.desc: What really happens during an out-of-body experience? Scientist Rebecca (Peri Gilpin) is so desperate to find out that she decides run an experiment on herself. But not everyone is happy of the lengths science will go to for empirical proof.

programme.title: The Sentence
programme.desc: Inventor Dr. Jack Henson (David Hyde Pierce) comes up with a "virtual prison" where criminals serve a life sentence in just a few, cost-efficient hours. When an innocent man's life is at stake, the doctor must submit himself to his own invention.

programme.title: Bits Of Love
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic underground bunker, a virtual reality world begins to turn on its inventor (Jon Tenney) after he becomes romantically involved with -- and then spurns -- the holographic interface (Natasha Henstridge) that controls the system.

programme.title: Second Thoughts
programme.desc: A mentally challenged man (Howie Mandel) is made more intelligent by a machine that transfers people's memories and experiences into his head, but kills its victims. And he soon discovers that the mind can be a minefield in The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Re-Generation

programme.title: La noche más fría
programme.desc: The story focuses on Carlos, a former fighter from the Malvinas (Faulkland islands). Carlos lives in a large city square, under a gazebo that he found in the neighborhood. He tragically lost his family in the War. Times were not kind to Carlos. While looking to reunite with his son, he sweeps sidewalks in exchange for tips to help him survive winter nights which have a punishing effect on Carlo's health. Preparing for the coldest night of the year, driven by the impulse to make it, Carlos packs few belongings to seek refuge in a municipal locality. However, the luck again avoids him. An incident at the door of the shelter leaves Carlos unfairly outside. Completely unprotected, away from his usual spot, without shelter and weak health. The coldest night of the year is looming, and Carlos must face endless adversities in an uneven fight against two cruel enemies: loneliness and cold. Thus begins the coldest night only fueled by the desire to survive.
